title: Python Basics - Your Foundation
description: Variables, data types, operators, and input/output with active AI tutoring

reading_too_long.message: 👋 Hey! I see you're reading about {topic}. Ready to write some code? Let me give you a simple exercise to try!

stuck_on_error.message: 🔍 I notice you're hitting the same error. Let me help! The issue is: {error_explanation}. Want me to show you the fix?

no_progress.message: 💭 Still thinking? No worries! Sometimes it helps to see an example first. Want me to show you one?

quick_success.message: ⚡ Wow, that was fast! You're getting good at this. Ready for a harder challenge?

high_cognitive_load.message: 💆 I can see this is a lot to process. Let's break it down into smaller chunks. Focus on just this part first: {simplified_step}

low_attention.message: 👀 Let's do something interactive! Quick question: What do you think this code will output? {code_snippet}

lessons.title: Variables - Your First Code!
lessons.ai_welcome_message: 👋 Welcome! I'm your AI tutor. I'll be watching your progress and jumping in to help whenever you need it. Let's learn variables together!

theory.introduction: Variables are like labeled boxes where you store information. Python remembers what you put in them so you can use it later!
theory.why_important: Without variables, you'd have to write the same data over and over. Variables let you store it once and reuse it anywhere in your program.

key_concepts.explanation: A variable is a name that refers to a value stored in computer memory. Think of it as a label on a box.
key_concepts.example: name = 'Alice'  # 'Alice' is stored in a box labeled 'name'
key_concepts.ai_hint: 💡 Try creating your own variable with your name! Just type: your_name = 'YourName'

key_concepts.explanation: Python has different types of data: strings (text), integers (whole numbers), floats (decimals), and booleans (True/False).
key_concepts.example:
age = 25          # Integer
height = 1.75     # Float
name = 'Bob'      # String
is_student = True # Boolean
key_concepts.ai_hint: 🎯 Notice how you don't need to tell Python the type? It figures it out automatically!

key_concepts.explanation: Variable names must start with a letter or underscore, can contain letters, numbers, and underscores, but no spaces or special characters.
key_concepts.example:
student_name = 'Emma'  # Good ✓
student1 = 'Bob'       # Good ✓
1st_student = 'Alex'   # Bad ✗ (starts with number)
student name = 'Sam'   # Bad ✗ (has space)
key_concepts.ai_hint: ⚠️ Common mistake: Don't start with numbers! Python will throw an error.

ai_reading_checkpoints.message: 📖 You're doing great reading! Want to pause and try writing a variable yourself?
�� That's a lot of theory! Let's make it real. I'll give you a simple task to try right now.

visual_diagram.title: How Variables Work - Step by Step
visual_diagram.content:
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  name = 'Alice'                     │
│  age = 25                           │
│  height = 1.65                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

STEP BY STEP:

1. CREATE variable 'name'
   ↓
   Computer creates a box labeled 'name'
   
2. STORE value 'Alice'
   ↓
   'Alice' goes into the box
   
   [name] → 'Alice'
   
3. USE the variable
   ↓
   print(name)
   ↓
   Output: Alice
   
4. CHANGE the value
   ↓
   name = 'Bob'
   ↓
   Old value replaced!
   
   [name] → 'Bob' (not 'Alice' anymore)


MULTIPLE VARIABLES:

[name]   → 'Alice'
[age]    → 25
[height] → 1.65

Each box is independent!
visual_diagram.ai_diagram_prompt: 👀 I see you're looking at the diagram. Want me to animate this for you? I can show you step-by-step what happens in memory!

complete_examples.title: Example 1: Personal Info Card
complete_examples.description: Store and display personal information in a formatted way
complete_examples.code:
# Store information
name = 'Emma Watson'
age = 25
city = 'London'
job = 'Developer'

# Display it nicely
print('=' * 40)
print(f'Name: {name}')
print(f'Age: {age} years old')
print(f'City: {city}')
print(f'Job: {job}')
print('=' * 40)
complete_examples.ai_explanation: 💡 See how we used f-strings (f'text {variable}')? This is the modern way to insert variables into text. Much cleaner than using + to join strings!
complete_examples.ai_challenge: 🎯 Now try changing the values to YOUR information and run it!

complete_examples.title: Example 2: Simple Calculator
complete_examples.code:
# Store two numbers
num1 = 10
num2 = 5

# Calculate
sum_result = num1 + num2
difference = num1 - num2
product = num1 * num2
division = num1 / num2

# Display results
print(f'{num1} + {num2} = {sum_result}')
print(f'{num1} - {num2} = {difference}')
print(f'{num1} × {num2} = {product}')
print(f'{num1} ÷ {num2} = {division}')
complete_examples.ai_explanation: 📊 Notice how division always returns a float (2.0) even when dividing evenly? Python does this to maintain precision!
complete_examples.ai_challenge: 🔥 Challenge: Add modulo (%) to show the remainder. Try: remainder = num1 % num2

complete_examples.title: Example 3: Temperature Converter
complete_examples.ai_explanation: 🌡️ This uses the formula: F = (C × 9/5) + 32. Math in Python works just like on paper!
complete_examples.ai_challenge: 💪 Can you add the reverse conversion? Hint: C = (F - 32) × 5/9

practice_exercises.title: About Me - Your First Program!
practice_exercises.description: Create variables for your name, age, and favorite hobby. Print them in a nice format!
practice_exercises.ai_intro: 🎯 Let's write your first real program! This is easy and fun. I'll guide you through it.
practice_exercises.starter_code:
# Create your variables here
name = 'Your Name'
age = 0
hobby = 'Your hobby'

# Print them nicely
print(f'Hi! I am {name}')
print(f'I am {age} years old')
print(f'I love {hobby}')

hints.message: 💭 Don't be shy! Just replace 'Your Name' with your actual name in quotes. Like: name = 'Emma'

🔍 Remember: strings need quotes, numbers don't!
   name = 'Emma'  ✓
   age = 25       ✓ (no quotes for numbers)

👀 Let me show you exactly what to do:

1. Change line 2: name = 'Emma' (or your name)
2. Change line 3: age = 25 (your age)
3. Change line 4: hobby = 'coding' (your hobby)

Try it now!

ai_on_run.success: 🎉 PERFECT! You just wrote your first Python program! See how easy that was? Ready for the next challenge?
ai_on_run.error: 🔍 Oops! I see an error: {error_message}. Let me help you fix it...
ai_on_run.partial: 👍 Good start! But I notice {issue}. Want a hint to fix it?

practice_exercises.solution:
name = 'Emma'
age = 25
hobby = 'coding'

print(f'Hi! I am {name}')
print(f'I am {age} years old')
print(f'I love {hobby}')
practice_exercises.ai_after_completion:
💪 Awesome work! You've learned:
✓ How to create variables
✓ How to use f-strings
✓ How to print output

Want to try something harder? I have a calculator challenge ready!

follow_up_challenge.message: 🔥 BONUS CHALLENGE: Add one more variable for your city and print it too!
follow_up_challenge.hint:
Add: city = 'London'
Then: print(f'I live in {city}')

practice_exercises.title: Rectangle Calculator
practice_exercises.description: Calculate the area and perimeter of a rectangle given width and height.
practice_exercises.ai_intro: 📐 Let's do some math with Python! You'll calculate area and perimeter. Don't worry, I'll help if you get stuck!
practice_exercises.starter_code:
# Rectangle dimensions
width = 8
height = 5

# Calculate area (width × height)
area = # Your code here

# Calculate perimeter (2 × (width + height))
perimeter = # Your code here

print(f'Area: {area}')
print(f'Perimeter: {perimeter}')

hints.message:
💡 Formula reminder:
   Area = width × height
   In Python: area = width * height

📝 For perimeter:
   Add width + height first
   Then multiply by 2
   perimeter = 2 * (width + height)

👀 Here's the solution:
   area = width * height
   perimeter = 2 * (width + height)

Type it out yourself - it helps you learn!

ai_on_error.forgot_multiplication: 🔍 I see you're adding width and height. That's for perimeter! For area, you need to MULTIPLY them: width * height
ai_on_error.syntax_error: ⚠️ Syntax error! Check your parentheses. Make sure they match: ( and )
ai_on_error.wrong_formula: 🤔 That formula doesn't look right. Remember: Area = width × height, Perimeter = 2 × (width + height)

practice_exercises.solution:
width = 8
height = 5

area = width * height
perimeter = 2 * (width + height)

print(f'Area: {area}')
print(f'Perimeter: {perimeter}')
practice_exercises.ai_after_completion:
🎊 Excellent! You're getting good at this! You just:
✓ Used variables in calculations
✓ Applied math formulas
✓ Formatted output nicely

Ready for a real-world example? Let's calculate a shopping bill with tax!

practice_exercises.title: Shopping Bill Calculator
practice_exercises.description: Calculate the total cost of 3 items including 20% tax. Display a receipt!
practice_exercises.ai_intro: 🛒 Now for something practical! You're building a real receipt calculator. This is what actual programs do!
practice_exercises.starter_code:
# Item prices
item1 = 29.99
item2 = 45.50
item3 = 12.75

# Calculate subtotal
subtotal = # Your code here

# Calculate tax (20%)
tax = # Your code here

# Calculate total
total = # Your code here

print('RECEIPT')
print('-' * 30)
print(f'Subtotal: £{subtotal:.2f}')
print(f'Tax (20%): £{tax:.2f}')
print(f'Total: £{total:.2f}')

hints.message:
🧮 Start with the easiest part:
   Subtotal = just add all three items together
   subtotal = item1 + item2 + item3

💰 Great! Subtotal is done. Now for tax:
   20% = 0.20 in decimal
   Tax = subtotal × 0.20
   tax = subtotal * 0.20

📊 Let me break it down:

1. subtotal = item1 + item2 + item3
2. tax = subtotal * 0.20
3. total = subtotal + tax

Try each line one at a time!

ai_progressive_feedback.after_subtotal: 👍 Subtotal looks good! Now calculate the tax (20% of subtotal).
ai_progressive_feedback.after_tax: 💪 Nice! Tax is correct. Final step: add subtotal + tax to get the total.
ai_progressive_feedback.all_correct: 🎉 PERFECT RECEIPT! You're ready to work at a shop!

practice_exercises.solution:
item1 = 29.99
item2 = 45.50
item3 = 12.75

subtotal = item1 + item2 + item3
tax = subtotal * 0.20
total = subtotal + tax

print('RECEIPT')
print('-' * 30)
print(f'Subtotal: £{subtotal:.2f}')
print(f'Tax (20%): £{tax:.2f}')
print(f'Total: £{total:.2f}')
practice_exercises.ai_after_completion:
🏆 AMAZING! You just built a real-world calculator! 

You've now mastered:
✓ Multiple variables
✓ Math operations
✓ Percentage calculations
✓ Formatted output with decimals

You're crushing it! Want to move to the next topic (Control Flow)?

bonus_challenge.title: 🔥 BONUS: Add a discount!
bonus_challenge.description: Add a 10% member discount BEFORE calculating tax
bonus_challenge.hint:
discount = subtotal * 0.10
subtotal_after_discount = subtotal - discount
Then calculate tax on the discounted subtotal!
